info.title: Document360 Customer API
info.description:
Document360 RESTful APIs will allow you to integrate your documentation with your software, allowing you to easily onboard new users, manage your articles and more.

You can find detailed API documentation here : [API Documentation](https://apidocs.document360.io/docs)

## Rate Limits
All endpoints are rate-limited per API token per project. Default limits vary by plan. When rate-limited, responses include `Retry-After`, `X-RateLimit-Limit`, `X-RateLimit-Remaining`, and `X-RateLimit-Reset` headers. Use exponential backoff with jitter for optimal retry behavior.

get.summary: Get AI search summary with total queries, answered/unanswered counts, and feedback.

429.description: Rate limit exceeded. Retry after the duration specified in the Retry-After header.

Retry-After.description: Number of seconds to wait before retrying the request. Use exponential backoff with jitter for optimal retry behavior.

X-RateLimit-Limit.description: The maximum number of requests allowed in the current time window. Limits are applied per API token per project.

X-RateLimit-Remaining.description: The number of requests remaining in the current time window. When this reaches 0, subsequent requests will receive a 429 response.

X-RateLimit-Reset.description: The UTC epoch timestamp (in seconds) when the current rate limit window resets.

get.summary: Get API reference logs.
get.description:
Returns a paginated list of import logs for the specified API reference. Each log represents
an import attempt (initial or re-import) and includes its success/failure status, error count,
and warning count. Logs are ordered by most recent first. Use the log detail endpoint to
retrieve the specific errors and warnings for a given log entry.

get.summary: Get errors and warnings for a specific log.
get.description:
Returns the detailed errors and warnings from a specific import log entry. Errors indicate
issues that prevented parts of the specification from being imported (e.g., invalid schema
definitions). Warnings indicate non-blocking issues that were auto-resolved or skipped
(e.g., unsupported extensions). Each entry includes the error/warning message and the
JSON pointer path to the affected location in the original specification.

parameters.description: The API reference identifier.

parameters.description: The log identifier.

post.summary: Import an API reference specification.
post.description:
Imports an API specification (OpenAPI/Swagger) from the provided URL into the specified project version.
The project version is validated before the import begins; a `422` is returned if the version does not exist
or is not enabled for API documentation. Requires the `UpdateArticles` permission. The import runs
asynchronously and returns a `202` with the new API reference ID and initial status.

Operation-Location.description: URL to poll for the status of the asynchronous operation.

Import accepted.summary: The API reference import has been accepted and is being processed.

get.summary: Get an article by ID.
get.description:
Returns the latest version of the article (draft or published). To retrieve a specific version,
use `GET /v3/projects/{projectId}/articles/{articleId}/versions/{versionNumber}` instead.
The response includes both raw content and rendered HTML.
For private or mixed-visibility projects, SAS tokens are appended to media URLs in the content by default.

parameters.name: content_mode
parameters.description: Controls content processing. Use `raw` (default) for unprocessed content with SAS tokens appended for private/mixed projects. Use `display` for fully rendered content with Snippets, Variables, and Glossaries resolved, SAS tokens appended, and internal notes removed. Warning: do not use display content for updates — Snippets, Variables, and Glossaries are resolved into inline HTML and the reusable component references will be lost.

schema.description: Controls how article/category content is processed in the response.

parameters.name: published
parameters.description: When true, returns the latest published version. When false (default), returns the latest version including drafts.

Article retrieved successfully.summary: Returns the latest version of the article with full content, authors, and available languages.

patch.description:
Performs a partial update on the article. Only fields included in the request body are modified;
omitted fields remain unchanged. When `auto_fork` is true and the target version is published,
a new draft version is automatically created and the update is applied to the draft.
If `auto_fork` is false (default) and you attempt to update content on a published version,
a 422 error is returned.

Update title and content with auto-fork.summary: Updates the article title and content. When auto_fork is true and the target version is published, a new draft version is created automatically.

independently — if one fails, others are still unpublished. The response includes per-article success/failure results.

get.summary: Get a specific category version.
get.description:
Retrieves the content for a specific version number of the category. Supports both `raw` and `display`
content modes, behaving identically to `GET /v3/projects/{projectId}/categories/{categoryId}/content`.
For private or mixed-visibility projects, SAS tokens are appended to media URLs in the content by default.
Returns `404` if the category exists but the requested version number does not. List available versions
via `GET /v3/projects/{projectId}/categories/{categoryId}/versions`.

parameters.name: content_mode
parameters.description: Controls content processing. Use `raw` (default) for unprocessed content with SAS tokens appended for private/mixed projects. Use `display` for fully rendered content with Snippets, Variables, and Glossaries resolved, SAS tokens appended, and internal notes removed. Warning: do not use display content for updates — Snippets, Variables, and Glossaries are resolved into inline HTML and the reusable component references will be lost.

schema.description: Controls how article/category content is processed in the response.

Category version retrieved successfully.summary: Returns the content for a specific version of the category.

get.summary: Get category content.
get.description:
Retrieves the markdown or HTML body content of a category.
For private or mixed-visibility projects, SAS tokens are appended to media URLs in the content by default.
When `contentMode` is set to `display`, Snippets, Variables, and Glossaries are resolved inline.
Set `published` to `true` to fetch only the latest published version; otherwise the
latest version including drafts is returned.

parameters.name: content_mode
parameters.description: Controls content processing. Use `raw` (default) for unprocessed content with SAS tokens appended for private/mixed projects. Use `display` for fully rendered content with Snippets, Variables, and Glossaries resolved, SAS tokens appended, and internal notes removed. Warning: do not use display content for updates — Snippets, Variables, and Glossaries are resolved into inline HTML and the reusable component references will be lost.

schema.description: Controls how article/category content is processed in the response.

parameters.name: published
parameters.description: When true, returns the latest published version. When false (default), returns the latest version including drafts.

Category content retrieved successfully.summary: Returns the content and versioning details for the specified category.

post.description:

if a draft version already exists for this category. The newly created draft can then be edited via
`PATCH /v3/projects/{projectId}/categories/{categoryId}/content`. The response includes the full content
of the new draft version.

get.summary: Search files and folders in drive.
get.description:
Performs a full-text search across file names in the project drive. Supports filtering by
`userIds`, date range (`filterFromDate`/`filterToDate`), `filterTags`,
and an `allowImagesOnly` flag. Results are paginated.
For private or mixed-visibility projects, file URLs include a time-limited SAS token by default.
Requires `ViewDrive` permission.

parameters.name: search_keyword
parameters.description: The text keyword to search for in file and folder names.

parameters.name: allow_images_only
parameters.description: When set to true, restricts results to image files only. Defaults to false.

parameters.name: user_ids
parameters.description: Filter results to files uploaded by the specified user identifiers.

parameters.name: filter_from_date
parameters.description: Filter results to files updated on or after this date (ISO 8601 format).

parameters.name: filter_to_date
parameters.description: Filter results to files updated on or before this date (ISO 8601 format).

parameters.name: filter_tags
parameters.description: Filter results to files tagged with the specified tag identifiers.

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor from a previous response's `next_cursor`. When provided, `page` is ignored.

parameters.name: include_total_count
parameters.description: Set to `true` to include `total_count` in the response. Default: `false`.

Search results.summary: Returns paginated search results for files matching the query

Requires the `ViewArticles` permission. The response is currently unpaginated and returns all matching articles.
If no articles match the specified status and language, an empty list is returned.

When the target version is already a draft, no fork occurs and the update is applied directly. When false (default), updating content on a published version returns a 422 error.

# Introduction
This updated guide walks you through configuring SSO.

category_id.description: The identifier of the category to move the article to. Retrieve category IDs from `GET /v3/projects/{projectId}/categories`.

hidden.description: Whether the article should be hidden from readers.

version_number.description: The version number to apply the update to. Defaults to the latest version if omitted. When `auto_fork` is true and this version is published, the update is applied to the newly created draft version, not the original published version.

translation_option.description: The updated translation status of the article. Possible values: 0 = None, 1 = NeedTranslation, 2 = Translated, 3 = InProgress.

source.description: The source of the update. Common values: `API`, `import`, `portal`. When omitted, defaults to `API` for Customer API requests.

order.description: The updated display order within the category.

auto_fork.description: When true and the target version is published, a new draft version is automatically forked and the update (including content and title) is applied to the new draft. When the target version is already a draft, no fork occurs and the update is applied directly. When false (default), updating content on a published version returns a 422 error.

Bearer.description: All V3 endpoints require a Bearer token. Generate tokens in the Document360 portal under **Settings > API Tokens**. Tokens are project-scoped, require the `customerApi` scope, and do not expire by default. Tokens can be revoked at any time from the portal. Include the token in every request: `Authorization: Bearer <your-token>`. Alternatively, use the Authorize button below to sign in via OAuth2 Authorization Code flow with PKCE.
